calendar.matters:
MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-25-626906 WITH CASE CGC-25-624900
Off Calendar May-26-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Cy Yee's unopposed Motion to Consolidate Cases CGC-25-626906 WITH CASE CGC-25-624900 is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing, the court orders the two captioned cases consolidated. The cases are consolidated for all purposes. CGC-25-624900 is the lead case and all pending dates, including trial and related dates, in that case apply to both cases.

Prior to the hearing time, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order quoting the text above verbatim.

No proposed order has been received.

Moving Party failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Christina Cicione In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Dec-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company's unopposed Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint is GRANTED.

"The court may, in furtherance of justice, and on any terms as may be proper, allow a party to amend any pleading or proceeding by adding or striking out the name of any party, or by correcting a mistake in the name of a party, or a mistake in any other respect; and may, upon like terms, enlarge the time for answer or demurrer. The court may likewise, in its discretion, after notice to the adverse party, allow, upon any terms as may be just, an amendment to any pleading or proceeding in other particulars; and may upon like terms allow an answer to be made after the time limited by this code." (Code Civil Procedure section 473(a)(1).) "[T]here is a strong policy in favor of liberal allowance of amendments." (Foxborough v. Van Atta (1994) 26 Cal.App.4th 217, 230.) "Great liberality is indulged in matters of amendment to the end that lawsuits may be determined upon their merits." (Desny v. Wilder (1956) 46 Cal.2d 715, 751.) "[I]t is an abuse of discretion to deny leave to amend where the opposing party was not misled or prejudiced by the amendment." (Kittredge Sports Co. v. Superior Court. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.) "It is irrelevant that new legal theories are introduced as long as the proposed amendments 'relate to the same general set of facts.'" (Id.)

Good cause appearing, Plaintiff's motion is granted. Plaintiff must file its first amended complaint forthwith, but no later than December 24, 2025.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
